creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to oleg from comment #3)

Hi Oleg,

Thank you for getting back to me.

The point is about using the standard variable names so extra stuff set by the user is taken into account.  You could for example do

    --with-cc-opt="${CFLAGS}"
    --with-ld-opt="${LDFLAGS}"

instead of using variables named CC_OPT and LD_OPT.  Then, USES=localbase should do the right thing.
